topic: stainless-steel fermentation and primary aroma preservation
trigger_keywords: preserva mejor los aromas frutales primarios

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Wine is fermented in an inert stainless-steel vessel rather than a flavour-active or oxygen-permeable container.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: The vessel contributes no wood flavour, limits oxygen exposure, and supports precise cool-temperature control.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Primary fruit aromas can remain clearer and fresher in the finished wine.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Inert, temperature-controlled stainless steel helps preserve primary fruit expression.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
